title: Cancel an external company
description: Tell CAIPO that an external (foreign) company is no longer doing business in Barbados.
summary: An external company is a company registered overseas that has been registered to do business in Barbados. If the company stops doing business in Barbados, the law requires it to file a notice of cancellation so the external company register stays accurate.
The relevant section of the Companies Act is Section 338. The notice removes the company from the register of external companies but does not affect its existence in its home country.
Before filing the notice, the directors usually pass a resolution approving the cancellation and prepare a statement of accounts covering activities up to the cancellation date.
Most external companies file the notice through a Barbadian attorney-at-law who has been acting as their local agent.

items: the address of the registered office in Barbados
the names and addresses of the directors
the date the company stopped doing business in Barbados

items.name: Statement of accounts up to the cancellation date
Photo ID for the signing officer
Proof of payment of the filing fee

fees: Filing fees are set by CAIPO. Confirm before you submit.
how_long: A decision usually takes 4 to 6 weeks.

options.heading: Through an attorney-at-law
content: A Barbadian attorney-at-law will file the notice with CAIPO.

contact.agency: Corporate Affairs and Intellectual Property Office (CAIPO)
contact.address:
Baobab Tower, Ground Floor
Warrens
St. Michael

assumptions: ‘Filing fees are set by CAIPO’ – we have not named a figure.
‘A decision usually takes 4 to 6 weeks’ is an estimate.
Document list (resolution, statement of accounts) is based on common CAIPO practice.
